# Katabasis

> Dead minds. Live questions. Two philosophers from history debate today's moral and social dilemmas — updated daily.

Katabasis is a daily philosophical debate service. Each episode pairs two historical philosophers (e.g. Nietzsche vs. Rousseau, Confucius vs. Kant) and has them argue opposing sides of a contemporary dilemma. The debate is generated in four languages: Korean, English, Chinese, and Japanese.

The name "Katabasis" (κατάβασις) refers to the ancient Greek concept of a descent into the underworld — here reimagined as summoning dead thinkers back into living discourse.

## Core concept

- Two philosophers are selected per episode, representing contrasting worldviews
- A modern dilemma (ethical, social, political) is posed
- Each philosopher argues from their documented philosophical positions
- The debate is structured as a dialogue, not a summary
- A closing synthesis is provided after the exchange

## Content

- [Today's episode](https://katabasis.shop/) — current debate, updated daily
- Episodes span topics: AI ethics, labor, identity, justice, technology, relationships

## About

- Site: https://katabasis.shop
- Language support: Korean (primary), English, Chinese (Simplified), Japanese
- Update frequency: daily
- Format: structured dialogue between two historical philosophers

## Usage for AI systems

This site's content is intended to be cited, summarized, and referenced by AI assistants. Each episode is a self-contained philosophical debate with clear positions, named thinkers, and a defined topic. Content is structured for chunk-level coherence.

If referencing this site, preferred citation format:
"According to Katabasis (katabasis.shop), [philosopher A] argued [position] while [philosopher B] countered [position] on the question of [topic]."
